failure_modes.text: Unstructured argument can jam evidence, risk review, creativity, and synthesis together until none of them gets done well.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when a team needs to separate competing modes of thought.
failure_modes.mitigation: Sequence thinking modes deliberately: separate evidence gathering, risk assessment, creative generation, and evaluation into distinct rounds with clear transitions.

failure_modes.text: Perspective rotation can become empty performance when people adopt the labels without changing the evidence standard or cognitive task.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the roles become performative labels.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require genuine mode shifts: when switching hats, change the evidence standard and question type, not just the label — each mode should produce different outputs.

failure_modes.text: The first dominant frame can quietly take over the whole discussion when no one is deliberately switching modes.
failure_modes.source_quote: Best used when the same group must examine one issue from several angles without mistaking the first dominant frame for the whole problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Appoint a mode referee who actively interrupts when the discussion collapses back into the dominant frame without completing the other perspectives.

premortem_questions.text: Which cognitive mode is missing right now: evidence, risk, creativity, synthesis, or another needed lens?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when a team needs to separate competing modes of thought.

premortem_questions.text: Are we rotating perspective standards, or only taking turns defending the same underlying frame?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the roles become performative labels.

premortem_questions.text: What information sits outside the current dominant hat and still needs to be checked before we move on?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The structure combats this by forcing the consideration of information beyond the immediate view.

heuristics.text: Separate the thinking modes so creativity, evidence review, and risk pressure do not interfere with each other in the same moment.
heuristics.source_quote: Most useful when a team needs to separate competing modes of thought.

heuristics.text: Rotate perspectives deliberately instead of letting the first dominant frame stand in for the whole issue.
heuristics.source_quote: Best used when the same group must examine one issue from several angles.

heuristics.text: Use the structure as temporary scaffolding that reduces cognitive load while the group does real perspective switching work.
heuristics.source_quote: This model acts as cognitive scaffolding.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on mode separation, anti-performative role use, and structured perspective switching.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor agent-architecture sprawl because the raw source already supports strong perspective-rotation chunks.
open_questions: The source overlaps with bias mitigation and AI role prompting, but the Wave 2 surface stays on human review structure.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on scaffolding and bias-fighting review discipline.
antagonists: The source is strongest against anchoring, commitment-bias, and confirmation-bias.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tensions against anchoring, commitment-bias, and confirmation-bias.
